t_ch: 伦纳德将探测仪对准了墙壁，墙上似乎浮现出了什么……
t_en: Leonard aims the detector at the wall. Something seems to appear on the wall...

t_ch: 呃，这是……
t_en: Uh... what exactly are—

t_ch: 一滴汗。
t_en: A drop of sweat.

t_ch: 怎么可能？为什么是…一滴汗？
t_en: Really? Why is there... a drop of sweat?

t_ch: 一滴水。
t_en: A drop of water.

t_ch: 一滴水？我怎么觉得…这更像是一滴汗？
t_en: A drop of water? Why do I feel like... it's more like a drop a sweat?

t_ch: 一个蓝色的水滴状物体。
t_en: A blue, teardrop-shaped object.

t_ch: 你在说什么啊！这怎么看都是…一滴汗？
t_en: What are you talking about!? This looks like... a drop of sweat?

t_ch: 她真好看。
t_en: Aaah... She's just so pretty.

t_ch: 没让你看这个！
t_en: I didn't ask you to look at that!

t_ch: 难以置信，空间站怎么会有这玩意儿？科员们就算再有想法，也不至于敢在墙上乱涂乱画，更何况还是…一滴汗？
t_en: Unbelievable. Why would something like this be in the space station? The staff wouldn't even dare scribble on the wall, let alone leave... a drop of sweat?

t_ch: 所以，这就是我们要找的…可疑痕迹？虽然和想象中不太一样……
t_en: So, this is what we're looking for... this suspicious evidence? It wasn't what I was expecting, but...

t_ch: 是挺可疑。
t_en: It is pretty suspicious.

t_ch: 是不太一样。
t_en: It's quite unexpected.

t_ch: 至少是个痕迹。
t_en: At least it's a clue.

t_ch: 你等一等，让我研究一下……
t_en: Wait a second... let me take a look...

t_ch: 哦，我懂了，这是一个电子涂鸦，倒是挺符合朋克洛德给人的印象。如果我没猜错，这应该是某种自动加密。
t_en: O—Oh, I see. This is digital graffiti that appears to represent Punklordian symbols. If I'm not mistaken, this should be some kind of automatic encryption.

t_ch: 咱们需要再破译一次，就能看到真正的可疑痕迹了。
t_en: We need to decipher it if want to find any culpatory evidence.

t_ch: 来试试吧。
t_en: Let me try.

t_ch: 给，把镜头对准墙上的涂鸦就行了。
t_en: Here — just point it at the graffiti on the wall.

t_ch: 跟我有什么关系？
t_en: What does this have to do with me?

t_ch: 呃，你倒是把我问住了，对话推进得太过自然，完全没意识到这个问题……
t_en: Ugh, you got me there. I got caught up in our conversation. I wasn't paying attention...

t_ch: 我想想，嗯…可能是我最近压力太大了，潜意识里希望有个同伴吧。而且你看，如果真能找到星核猎手的线索，对你也有帮助吧？
t_en: Let me think... Um, maybe I've been under too much stress recently and I subconsciously want a friend. Anyway, if I were to find clues related to the Stellaron Hunters, wouldn't that be helpful to you too?

t_ch: 总之，就当是帮我个忙吧！不会占用你太多时间的。给，把镜头对准墙上的涂鸦就行了。
t_en: Ugh, just do me a favor! It won't take up too much of your time. Here — just point it at the graffiti on the wall.

t_ch: 奇怪，应该就在这附近啊，该不会是藏在黑塔女士的画像里吧……
t_en: Strange... it should be around here somewhere. Maybe it's hidden inside Madam Herta's portrait...

t_ch: 嗯？有什么事吗，找前台的话，出门笔直走就是…咦，是{NICKNAME}啊！好久不见啊！
t_en: Huh? Do you need something? If you're looking for the front desk, just go straight... Oh! {NICKNAME}! Long time no see!

t_ch: 好久不见啊！
t_en: Long time no see!

t_ch: 没想到你也在这儿——不会又收到了《太空大战争》的短信吧，哈哈。
t_en: I didn't expect you to be here! Did you get another Cosmic Warfare message? Haha!

t_ch: 最近过得如何？
t_en: How have you been?

t_ch: 嗐，不怎么样，你看，还是老样子，和《太空大战争》那会儿没什么区别……
t_en: *sigh* Not so good. Still the same as before... just like during Cosmic Warfare...

t_ch: 请问你是…
t_en: Sorry, you are...?

t_ch: 啊？你不会把我忘了吧…《太空大战争》，还记得吗？
t_en: Huh? You don't remember me...? Cosmic Warfare, remember?

t_ch: 我想起来了！
t_en: I remember!

t_ch: 对吧，我就知道你不会忘记的。
t_en: Yeah! I knew you wouldn't forget me.

t_ch: 我还是想不起来…
t_en: I don't remember...

t_ch: 啊？好吧，贵人多忘事，我帮你回忆一下——
t_en: Huh? Well, you've got a lot on your plate. Let me jog your memory...

t_ch: 先做个自我介绍。我叫伦纳德，是这座空间站的网络安全工程师。
t_en: Allow me to introduce myself. I'm Leonard, a cybersecurity engineer here at the space station.

t_ch: 你应该知道吧？前不久，这里遭到了「反物质军团」的袭击，在那场骚乱中，各舱段的主控系统也瘫痪了。
t_en: You probably know that this place was recently attacked by the Antimatter Legion. In the chaos, the master control system for each zone was paralyzed.

t_ch: 现在空间站正在进行灾后重建，我的职责就是协助修复各种设备，恢复空间站的网络安全。
t_en: Now that the station is being rebuilt, my job is to repair equipment and restore the space station's cybersecurity.

t_ch: 不过在修复过程中，我却发现了一些异常——访问记录显示，在主控系统瘫痪的那段时间，有「军团」以外的第三者入侵了这里。
t_en: But during the repair process, I discovered some anomalies. The access records show that a third party other than the Legion invaded while the master control system was paralyzed.

t_ch: 我们怀疑来者是「星核猎手」。这群人神出鬼没，危险得很，个个在「公司」通缉中榜上有名。
t_en: We suspect that it may have been the Stellaron Hunters. It's an elusive group, and very dangerous. Every member is on the IPC's wanted list.

t_ch: 其中还有个来自朋克洛德的超级骇客，说来惭愧，我已经在她那儿吃过一次亏了——
t_en: One of them is a super hacker from Punklorde. I'm ashamed to admit that I've lost to her once before...

t_ch: 前阵子，我在筛查过程中发现了她的行踪，本打算顺藤摸瓜找出对方的底细，却被反将一军，中了她的陷阱。
t_en: A while ago, I discovered her whereabouts during a screening process. I was planning to follow the clues to find more details about her, but I fell into her trap.

t_ch: 你猜后来发生了什么？她反向骇入我的设备，给一堆人发了垃圾短信，整了个面向全空间站的游戏，告诉所有人有个叫伦纳德的呆瓜闯了大祸……
t_en: Can you guess what happened next? She reverse-hacked my device, sent out a bunch of spam, made a video game open to the space station, and told everyone that an idiot named Leonard made a big, silly mistake...

t_ch: 怎么样，想起来了吗？我说的应该够详细了。
t_en: Well? Do you remember me now? That should be enough to jog your memory.

t_ch: 哦对，你可能还不知道。那起事故已经平息了，我本以为自己会被追责，但黑塔女士大人有大量，竟然允许我继续调查，真是不幸中的万幸。
t_en: Oh, maybe you haven't heard. Now that everything has calmed down, I thought they'd be on my case already. The silver lining? Madam Herta was generous enough to allow me to continue investigating this matter.

t_ch: 恭喜啊。
t_en: Congratulations.

t_ch: 嗯，我也没想到。空间站的事务通常都是艾丝妲站长在打理，我还是第一次见黑塔女士亲自下指令。
t_en: Yeah, it was pretty unexpected. Space station affairs are usually handled by Lead Researcher Asta — this is the first time I've seen Madam Herta give the orders in person.

t_ch: 是不想管吧。
t_en: Normally she doesn't want to deal with it.

t_ch: 所以才说是不幸中的万幸呀……黑塔女士的作风你也知道，她要真对什么东西感兴趣，就没别人什么事儿了。
t_en: Hence my "silver lining..." You know Madam Herta — if she's really interested in something, it doesn't matter what anyone else says.

t_ch: 是在给你穿小鞋吧。
t_en: She's getting revenge on you.

t_ch: 没、没这么夸张吧…我算什么东西，还轮得到黑塔女士来惦记？
t_en: I—I wouldn't go that far... I'm just a nobody. You think Madam Herta would remember me?

t_ch: 总之，领导都给机会了，我怎能不珍惜？要是能将功补过，好好表现一番，我说不定还有扭转风评的机会。
t_en: To put it simply, the leaders have given me an opportunity. How could I not cherish it? If I can make up for my mistakes and do a good job, I may have a chance to prove myself.

t_ch: 哦，说了这么多，还没跟你解释我在这儿做什么——刚才也提到，我正在继续调查这件事。
t_en: Oh, I haven't properly explained to you what I'm doing here... As I mentioned, I'm investigating this situation.

t_ch: 这次我打算从空间站内部入手，看看能不能找到一些线索，还原入侵者当时的行动路线，说不定就能发现之前错过的盲区。
t_en: This time, I plan to start from inside the space station and see if I can find some clues to trace the intruders' steps. Maybe I can find the blind spot I missed before.

t_ch: 我设计了一种解码程序，看——虽然长得像个相机，但这其实是个探测仪。透过它，我们就能看到一些被隐藏起来的痕迹。
t_en: I designed a decoding program. Look — it looks like a camera, but it's actually a detector. We should be able to find hidden clues with it.

t_ch: 就像…这样！
t_en: Just like... this!

t_ch: 伦纳德将探测仪对准了墙壁，墙上似乎浮现出了什么……
t_en: Leonard aims the detector at the wall. Something seems to appear on the wall...

t_ch: 呃，这是……
t_en: Uh, that's...

t_ch: 一滴汗。
t_en: A drop of sweat.

t_ch: 怎么可能？为什么是…一滴汗？
t_en: Really? Why is there... a drop of sweat?

t_ch: 一滴水。
t_en: A drop of water.

t_ch: 一滴水？我怎么觉得…这更像是一滴汗？
t_en: A drop of water? Why do I feel like... it's more like a drop a sweat?

t_ch: 一个蓝色的水滴状物体。
t_en: A blue, teardrop-shaped object.

t_ch: 你在说什么啊！这怎么看都是…一滴汗？
t_en: What are you talking about!? This looks like... a drop of sweat?

t_ch: 她真好看。
t_en: Aaah... She is pretty.

t_ch: 没让你看这个！
t_en: I didn't ask you to look at that!

t_ch: 难以置信，空间站怎么会有这玩意儿？科员们就算再有想法，也不至于敢在墙上乱涂乱画，更何况还是…一滴汗？
t_en: Unbelievable. Why would something like this be in the space station? The staff wouldn't even dare scribble on the wall, let alone leave... a drop of sweat?

t_ch: 所以，这就是我们要找的…可疑痕迹？虽然和想象中不太一样……
t_en: So, this is what we're looking for... this suspicious evidence? It wasn't what I was expecting, but...

t_ch: 是挺可疑。
t_en: It is pretty suspicious.

t_ch: 是不太一样。
t_en: It's quite unexpected.

t_ch: 至少是个痕迹。
t_en: At least it's a clue.

t_ch: 你等一等，让我研究一下……
t_en: Wait a second... let me take a look...

t_ch: 哦，我懂了，这是一个电子涂鸦，倒是挺符合朋克洛德给人的印象。如果我没猜错，这应该是某种自动加密。
t_en: O—Oh, I see. This is digital graffiti that appears to represent Punklordian symbols. If I'm not mistaken, this should be some kind of automatic encryption.

t_ch: 咱们需要再破译一次，就能看到真正的可疑痕迹了。
t_en: We need to decipher it if want to find any culpatory evidence.

t_ch: 来试试吧。
t_en: Let me try.

t_ch: 给，把镜头对准墙上的涂鸦就行了。
t_en: Here — just point it at the graffiti on the wall.

t_ch: 跟我有什么关系？
t_en: What does this have to do with me?

t_ch: 呃，你倒是把我问住了，对话推进得太过自然，完全没意识到这个问题……
t_en: Ugh, you got me there. I got caught up in our conversation. I wasn't paying attention...

t_ch: 我想想，嗯…可能是我最近压力太大了，潜意识里希望有个同伴吧。而且你看，如果真能找到星核猎手的线索，对你也有帮助吧？
t_en: Let me think... Um, maybe I've been under too much stress recently and I subconsciously want a friend. Anyway, if I were to find clues related to the Stellaron Hunters, wouldn't that be helpful to you too?

t_ch: 总之，就当是帮我个忙吧！不会占用你太多时间的。给，把镜头对准墙上的涂鸦就行了。
t_en: Ugh, just do me a favor! It won't take up too much of your time. Here — just point it at the graffiti on the wall.

t_ch: 你用镜头对准墙上的涂鸦，蓝色的图案开始闪烁你从未见过的纹路。
t_en: You pointed it at the graffiti on the wall, then some blinking blue patterns started to appear

t_ch: 突然，你看着它开始向内坍缩，周围的声音、色彩都被卷入其中，似乎有什么东西呼之欲出……
t_en: Suddenly, the pattern imploded and engulfed its surrounding sounds and colors. Something inside seems to be breaking out from it...

t_ch: 啊咿——这里怎么会有虚卒？！
t_en: Ahhhh! Why are there Voidrangers here!?

t_ch: 吓、吓死我了！你看到了吗，啪的一下，这里好像冒出了什么东西，然后虚卒就跳出来了！
t_en: Th—That scared me to death! Did you see that? It seemed like something popped up here, then Voidrangers jumped out!

t_ch: 冷静，都解决了。
t_en: Don't worry. It's taken care of.

t_ch: 吓死我了！
t_en: That scared me to death!

t_ch: 好像多了个人…
t_en: There seems to be an extra person...

t_ch: 但是你看！和我猜的一样，这涂鸦果然是道加密，破译之后，咱们就能看到被隐藏起来的信息。
t_en: But look! Just like I guessed, this graffiti is encrypted! If we decipher it, we can see some hidden information.

t_ch: 监控拍到了她的样子，「银狼」，和公司通缉令上的照片一模一样。让我看看她在做什么……
t_en: The surveillance camera captured her face — "Silver Wolf." It's exactly the same as the photo on the IPC's wanted list. Let me see what she was up to...

t_ch: 这是，一个传输信标？哦，我明白了，她应该是在入侵时和反物质军团撞了个满怀，就用这个信标把敌人传送去了什么地方。
t_en: Is this... a transmitter beacon? Oh, I see! She probably ran into the Antimatter Legion during the invasion, then used this beacon to teleport the enemy somewhere else.

t_ch: 刚才我们无意中再次激活了这个信标，它们就被丢了回来…我的天啊，星核猎手还有这种本事？
t_en: We accidentally activated the beacon again just now, and they were brought back... Geez, Stellaron Hunters have the ability to stow enemies away like that?

t_ch: 点对点，不需要动力源支持的，单凭计算实现的信标传送？难怪他们能攻入空间站…有这种能力，恐怕连黑塔女士的办公室都不安全……
t_en: Beacon transmission that doesn't require a power source and can be implemented solely through computation? No wonder they were able to break into the space station... With this, I'm afraid not even Madam Herta's office is safe...

t_ch: 别灰心。
t_en: Don't be discouraged.

t_ch: 这真的很难冷静啊，你看，要不是有你在，我这会儿估计已经躺地上了。
t_en: It's really hard to stay calm... Y'know, if it wasn't for you, I'd probably be lying on the ground right now.

t_ch: 所以咱们一起行动真的很重要，拜托你了！
t_en: So it's really important that we work together! Please!

t_ch: 这种技术很厉害吗？
t_en: Is this kind of tech really that impressive?

t_ch: 岂止是厉害，怎么说呢，就像是…她能把「现实」当作程序一样自由编辑。
t_en: It's not only impressive — how should I put it... She can edit "reality" as freely as she could edit a program.

t_ch: 这事听着就很离谱，对不对？我一直以为这是项，呃，酒吧科学，除了假面愚者，没人相信它真的存在。
t_en: Sounds unbelievable, doesn't it? I always thought it was fringe science, and that nobody other than the Masked Fools believed it really existed.

t_ch: 相信我们能解决。
t_en: Have some faith — we'll figure this out.

t_ch: 唉，你真乐观，我就不一样，你看，要不是有你在，我这会儿估计已经躺地上了。
t_en: Well, I don't share your optimism. If it wasn't for you, I'd probably be lying on the ground right now.

t_ch: 总之，咱们一起努力吧……
t_en: Anyway, let's work together...

t_ch: 现在看来，这个房间就是入侵的起点，我们继续追踪吧。
t_en: It looks like this room was the initial starting point of the invasion. Let's follow the trail.

t_ch: 哦对了，得把黑塔女士的全息映射关掉，搜查是个大工程，万一动了什么不该动的就惨了……
t_en: Oh, by the way — Madam Herta's holographic projection has to be turned off. This search is a big undertaking. If something were to be moved out of place, it could ruin the investigation...

t_ch: 附近有个奇怪的信号，可能是另一个涂鸦。
t_en: There's a strange signal nearby. Maybe it's another piece of graffiti.

t_ch: 看，在前面——这家伙还标个问号，像是在拿我们寻开心。
t_en: Look — up there. Tsk, they even put down a question mark — they must be making fun of us.

t_ch: 她往基座舱段的方向去了，也对，只有这一条路嘛。
t_en: She went toward the base zone... Yep, there's only one way.

t_ch: 唉，又不见了？这玩意儿解码起来断断续续的。
t_en: Ugh, disappeared again? This thing keeps cutting out when I'm deciphering it.

t_ch: 她应该在这儿停留过一段时间，四处找找吧。
t_en: She must have stopped here for a bit. Let's look around.

t_ch: 看这个方向，她接下来和同伴一起去了监控室。
t_en: They seem to be heading toward the monitoring room.

t_ch: 又是一个涂鸦，竟然每个都不一样，她可真有心思。
t_en: Another graffiti... They're all different too. She really put some thought into this.

t_ch: 呃，这段记录是不是损坏了——她穿过墙，然后消失了？
t_en: Huh, is this section of the record damaged...? She went through the wall and disappeared.

t_ch: 她篡改了记录？
t_en: She altered the records?

t_ch: 不太可能。若真是如此，我们之前看到的都可能是假的。
t_en: That's highly unlikely, but if it's true, everything we've seen till now could be a fake.

t_ch: 她传送了自己？
t_en: She teleported herself?

t_ch: 不太可能，你看，她有个穿过墙的过程，很明显。
t_en: I don't think so, that's not the same as passing straight through a wall — look.

t_ch: 是不是显示故障？
t_en: Could it be a display malfunction?

t_ch: 不太可能，这探测仪我上周才买，还在退款有效期内呢。
t_en: That's highly unlikely, I bought this detector just last week. The refund guarantee hasn't even expired yet.

t_ch: 你不是说是自己研发的嘛…
t_en: Didn't you say you developed this yourself...?

t_ch: 呃…晚点再讨论这个。
t_en: Uhh... Let's talk about that later.

t_ch: 等下等下，我知道了！这应该是奇物的效果，这里原本有一段预设好的折叠空间，估计被入侵者发现并利用了。
t_en: Hold on, I got it! It might be an effect from a curio. There was a pre-established folded space here, and the intruders found it and took advantage of it.

t_ch: 怪不得她之前一直在翻奇物收藏目录。这段空间连接着星核收藏室，还记得吗？他们就是在那儿把星核放入你体内的。
t_en: No wonder she kept going through the curio collection index. This space is connected to the Stellaron room. Do you still remember it? That's where they put the Stellaron inside you.

t_ch: 我没有印象了。
t_en: I can't recall.

t_ch: 正常的。 这也只是我的主观推断。
t_en: Well, that's not unusual. Anyway, this is all just an assumption of mine.

t_ch: 好像记得这个场景。
t_en: I think I remember this.

t_ch: 卡芙…卡……
t_en: Kaf... ka...

t_ch: 啊？你在念叨什么呢？
t_en: Huh? What are you muttering about?

t_ch: 不过…如果这伙人的目的是星核，那又为什么要把你留在这里呢？以她的实力，带着星核逃脱不就是多打个信标的事情吗？
t_en: Hmm... Why would they leave you here if their objective was the Stellaron? With her capabilities, all she would've needed to do was make another beacon — she would've been able to escape with the Stellaron no problem.

t_ch: 唔，总觉得我们追了很久，却没找到什么有用的信息…不过这也正常，万事开头难嘛，我们再去收容舱段看下。
t_en: *sigh* I feel like we've been chasing for a long time and still haven't found anything useful... Oh well, I suppose that's the way it goes — things are more difficult at the outset. Let's take another look at the storage zone.

t_ch: 啊…这里竟然没有涂鸦？完了，线索断了……
t_en: Huh...? There's no graffiti here... We're doomed. The trail has gone cold...

t_ch: 这也太奇怪了，难道她真的就这样走了？千里迢迢来到这里，最后却什么都没带走，也什么都没有留下，这么潇洒的吗？
t_en: So strange... Did she really leave just like that? She came all this way and didn't take anything with her, nor leave anything behind.

t_ch: 哦不，他们留下了你…呃，这么说有些奇怪，我的意思是，在一个完全陌生的地方醒来，什么都不知道的感觉一定不好受，对吧？
t_en: Oh, uh... they did leave you behind. Ahem, sorry, that sounded better in my head... It must've been a terrible feeling waking up in a place that was completely unfamiliar to you...

t_ch: 没关系。
t_en: That's alright.

t_ch: 还好吧。
t_en: It wasn't so bad.

t_ch: 听起来你深有体会。
t_en: Sounds like you've experienced it yourself.

t_ch: 我吗？我每天早上起床时都有这种感觉。
t_en: Me? That's how I feel when I wake up every day.

t_ch: 总觉得他们的目的没这么单纯。唉，多希望能找到一些对你有用的线索，不然让你白忙活这么久，真挺不好意思的。
t_en: I can't help but feel that there's more that they're after. *sigh* I really hope I can find something that's useful to you... otherwise I'll feel bad for having you do so much work for nothing.

t_ch: 唔，感觉越说越心虚了，我还是找点自己能做的事吧…看，这儿有个定分枪，我还不知道自己能在它这儿拿多少分……
t_en: *sigh* ...The more I talk about it, the worse I feel. I should probably find something that I can do... Hmm, there's a rating pistol here. I wonder what my rating would be...

t_ch: 咦！等一下，快看这边！定分枪的收容舱有一条奇怪的访问记录。
t_en: Whoa! Look here! There's a strange access log in the rating pistol's compartment.

t_ch: 看时间，就是军团入侵那会儿，科员们应该都疏散了才对，所以是她？她没有马上离开，在把星核放入你体内后，还留在这里玩了一会儿定分枪？
t_en: It looks like the log time occurred during the Legion's invasion — the researchers would've already been evacuated by then. Could it have been her? In which case, she didn't leave immediately... She implanted the Stellaron inside you and then fiddled around with the rating pistol for a while?

t_ch: 难道…{NICKNAME}，我突然意识到一件事，我们得去趟另一侧的奇物收藏室。
t_en: Could it be that... {NICKNAME}, I just realized something. We need to go to the Curio collection room on the other side.

t_ch: 果然…她来过这里！
t_en: Just as I thought... She was here!

t_ch: 我才想起来，在黑塔女士的众多藏品中，有一张叫做「朋克洛德精神」的游戏卡带。
t_en: I just remembered that Madam Herta has a game cartridge named "Punklorde Mentality" in her collection.

t_ch: 游戏卡带？
t_en: Game cartridge?

t_ch: 朋克洛德？
t_en: Punklorde?

t_ch: 好像在模拟宇宙见过…
t_en: I think I've seen it in the Simulated Universe...

t_ch: 嗯，你也有印象吧？事实上，它也在下落不明的奇物清单里。
t_en: You remember it, right? It's actually on the list of missing curios.

t_ch: 之前没把这两件事联系在一起，但如果那位骇客在完成星核猎手的任务之外，还怀着自己的个人目的——
t_en: I wasn't able to put the two together before. If the hacker has a personal objective other than the Stellaron Hunter's mission...

t_ch: 我明白了！我明白了！星核猎手的行动，不过是她的障眼法！
t_en: I got it! I got it now! The Stellaron Hunters' operation was just a cover for her!

t_ch: 奇物才是她的真正目标？
t_en: She was actually after the curio?

t_ch: 没错！
t_en: That's right!

t_ch: 感觉本末倒置了吧。
t_en: Feels like you got it backwards.

t_ch: 从结果上来看，这群家伙根本没有带走星核。
t_en: Look, in the end, they didn't take the Stellaron with them.

t_ch: 她之所以那么关心奇物收藏目录，任务完成后依然逗留在空间站，是因为这才是她的目标！
t_en: She was so interested in the curio collection index that she stayed in the space station even after her mission was complete — this was what she was truly after!

t_ch: 这样星核猎手的行动，反而会成为我们追查时的烟雾弹。厉害啊，不愧是朋克洛德的家伙。
t_en: She knew we would be looking for the Stellaron Hunters and used it as her cover. Very smart — to be expected of someone from Punklorde.

t_ch: 哎等下，有个说不通的地方。奇物是黑塔女士最爱惜的东西了，所有的访问申请都会通过主控室的电脑发送到她的办公室。
t_en: Hold on, though... Something doesn't add up. The curios are Madam Herta's most prized possessions. All the visit requests are sent to her office from the computer in the main control room.

t_ch: 之前有科员没有报备，想偷偷用奇物做研究，全被她发现了。
t_en: Some researchers tried to gain access to the curios for research purposes once, but they didn't get proper authorization... She caught them all red-handed.

t_ch: 有种不好的预感。
t_en: I have a bad feeling about this.

t_ch: 我也是。如果黑塔女士没有反应，就只有一种可能…
t_en: Me too. The fact Madam Herta hasn't responded to this can only mean one thing...

t_ch: 那就是主控室也被骇入了。{NICKNAME}，我们应该过去确认一下。
t_en: ...that the main control room was hacked as well. {NICKNAME}, we need to go and take a look.

t_ch: 总不会主控室也被入侵了吧？
t_en: Could it be that the main control room was hacked as well?

t_ch: 把我给问住了。但…以这位骇客的技术，想骇入主控室应该不是问题。
t_en: You got me there. But... with the skills of this hacker, I do think it's possible.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我们应该去主控室确认一下。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, we should go and check the main control room.

t_ch: 比科员更爱惜吗?
t_en: She values them even more than the researchers?

t_ch: 那肯定…比我重要得多吧？不插科打诨了，{NICKNAME}，我们应该去主控室看一下。
t_en: Well... they're definitely far more important than I am. But enough of that. {NICKNAME}, we should go check out the main control room.

t_ch: 等下，你先别动！我现在看到空间站的电子屏幕就浑身发毛，让我先检查一下……
t_en: Wait, don't move! I get chills just looking at the space station monitors now. Let me investigate first...

t_ch: 好极了，岂止有访问记录，整个终端传输都被她关闭了——我之前还以为是军团造成的。
t_en: Just perfect. Not only did she shut off all the access logs, she shut off all the terminal transmissions too... I thought it was the Legion that did this.

t_ch: 要重新开启吗？
t_en: Do we need to do a restart?

t_ch: 还是先做正事吧。
t_en: Let's focus on the main task first.

t_ch: 这么久都没人发现？
t_en: No one noticed it this whole time?

t_ch: 我感觉是这事优先级比较低，空间站的待办事项都堆到天上去了。
t_en: This wouldn't have been a high-priority issue. The space station's to-do list is ridiculously long at this point.

t_ch: 不过好奇怪，虽然她暂停了终端传输，但本地数据却一点也没动。我是真的完全搞不懂这家伙了。
t_en: I am curious though. She paused the terminal transmissions, but didn't touch the local data at all. I really don't get her.

t_ch: 身为星核猎手，却不带走星核；身为骇客，却不删除本地记录？哎，这边有个外来接口，我接入调查一下。
t_en: As a Stellaron Hunter, she leaves the Stellaron behind. As a hacker, she doesn't delete the local log. Hmm, there's an external port here. I'm gonna plug in and take a look.

t_ch: 不好的预感…
t_en: I have a bad feeling about this...

t_ch: 可能是陷阱…
t_en: It might be a trap...

t_ch: 不如让我来吧…
t_en: Why don't you let me do it...

t_ch: 放轻松，朋友，我伦纳德做这个很熟练的，你看这个协议——
t_en: Relax, friend. I've done this a million times. Look at this agreement...

t_ch: 正如你担心的那样，一阵光晕开，敌人出现了……
t_en: As you feared, a burst of light shines and the enemy appears...

t_ch: 你看我说的对吧！这家伙留着的本地数据果然有蹊跷！
t_en: See? I was right! There was something fishy about the local data she left behind!

t_ch: 果然有蹊跷！
t_en: So there was something strange after all!

t_ch: 你还是别说话了…
t_en: Yeah, yeah...

t_ch: 现在可以查阅奇物的出入记录了…咦，最离谱的事发生了，这件奇物好像压根就没离开空间站。
t_en: Now we can browse through the curio access logs... Huh? That can't be right. This curio doesn't seem to have ever left the space station.

t_ch: 她也没带走奇物？
t_en: She didn't take the curio either?

t_ch: 凭空消失了？
t_en: It just disappeared into thin air?

t_ch: 呃，从结果而言，是的。记录显示，这件奇物最后被送进了…黑塔女士的办公室？然后就消失了？
t_en: Hmm, from the way things look, yes. The records show that this curio was last sent to... Madam Herta's office? And then it disappeared?

t_ch: 我已经晕了，这家伙到底是来干嘛的？总不见得这些涂鸦真的是耍我们玩儿的吧，不会吧？
t_en: I'm seriously confused. What did she come here for? She wouldn't have put the graffiti up just to mess with us, would she?

t_ch: 唔，不行，这样想就输了。就算只剩最后一条线索也要排查下去，不然，我的工作可能真的要不保了……
t_en: *sigh* No, if I start thinking like that I may as well give up now. I have to fully investigate every lead or I'll really be out of a job...

t_ch: 只有一个办法了，接下来，我们去黑塔女士的办公室看看吧！
t_en: We have no choice but to check Madam Herta's office next!

t_ch: 支援舱段检修完了吗？这周必须得收工，不能再拖了。
t_en: Are the repairs in the supply zone finished? We have to wrap them up this week. No more delays.

t_ch: 差、差不多了吧，就剩几台压缩机了……
t_en: A—Almost... there are just a few compressors left...

t_ch: 支援舱段检修完了吗……
t_en: Are the repairs in the supply zone finished...

t_ch: 只…只剩压缩机了……
t_en: J—Just a few compressors left...

s_en: Researcher of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 你这维修报表再拿去核对一下吧…不是我说，你自己数数上面有几个零。
t_en: Take this maintenance report of yours and look it over again. You need to count the zeros yourself — that goes without saying.

s_en: Researcher of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 还有这边， 小号安抚型毛绒鲨鱼？这又是什么东西…收容室里有这玩意儿吗？
t_en: And here — what the heck is a "small stuffed animal shark for emotional support"? This kind of stuff is in storage?

s_en: Researcher of the Department of Implement Arts
t_ch: 这份维修报表…再拿去核对一下吧……
t_en: This maintenance report... shall look it over again...

t_ch: 怎么突然来了一堆人，大家都不用工作吗？
t_en: What are all these people doing here all of a sudden? Don't they have work to do?

t_ch: 不清楚，好像是有重要的客人来访。
t_en: Not sure. I heard there's an important guest coming.

t_ch: 他们都是来迎接的？不像咱们空间站的风格啊，之前公司的事故核查部门过来都没这么大阵仗。
t_en: They're all here to welcome a guest? That's pretty strange for our space station. Even the IPC's Incident Inspection Department never received a welcome party like this.

t_ch: 怎么突然来了一堆人啊……
t_en: What are all these people doing here...

t_ch: 好像是有重要的客人来访……
t_en: An important guest coming...

t_ch: 「这次能让我玩得开心点吗…之前的几次行动都很无聊呢……」
t_en: Can you let me have some fun this time? Our last few operations turned out to be pretty dull.

t_ch: 这家伙真是游刃有余啊，竟然在看奇物收藏目录？
t_en: She makes it seem easy... Is she really looking at the curio collection index?

t_ch: 有声音了。
t_en: There's a voice.

t_ch: 断断续续的…
t_en: It's cutting out...

t_ch: 她在和谁说话？
t_en: Who is she talking to?

t_ch: 看这幅悠然自得的样子，她应该在这个房间待了好一会儿，估计是在等什么人吧。不知道之后发生了什么，咱们再看看别的地方。
t_en: Judging by how calm she is, she must have been in this room for a while — maybe she was waiting for someone. I'm not sure what happened after that — let's search somewhere else.

t_ch: 「走吧…假身份信息已经录入……」
t_en: Your fake ID info is ready...

t_ch: 原来如此，自己先潜入，然后帮同伴走正门，很经典的骇客操作。我看看，登录的名字是…「卡里维尔•伦纳德」？！
t_en: So, that's what happened! She sneaked in first, then helped her partner in through the main door. It's a classic hacking operation. Let's see... The registered name is... "Leonard Calliwell"!?

t_ch: 你是内鬼！
t_en: Aha! You're the imposter! Among Us!

t_ch: 怎么可能！是、是她盗用了我的身份信息！
t_en: How is this possible!? Sh—She used my identity!

t_ch: 一眼真。
t_en: I knew you were sus from the beginning!

t_ch: 她、她竟然盗用了我的身份信息？！
t_en: Sh—She used my identity!?

t_ch: 谁？
t_en: Leonard call who well?

t_ch: 这是我的名字啊！她盗用了我的身份信息！
t_en: That's my name! She used my identity!

t_ch: 这，例行检查时我都没发现这条记录有问题，看到自己名字就下意识略过了…太狡猾了！
t_en: I—I never noticed that there was an issue with this record. I subconsciously glanced right past it when I saw my own name... She's so cunning!

t_ch: 太狡猾了！
t_en: She's a sneaky one!

t_ch: 没错！
t_en: Right!

t_ch: 要不要先反思一下自己…
t_en: Maybe you need to "check yo' self before you wreck yo' self"

t_ch: 百、百密一疏啊，正常人第一反应都是去查不认识的名字，谁会想到问题出在自己身上？
t_en: I—It's an honest mistake. The reaction of any ordinary person is to check for unfamiliar names, right? Who would think to check their own name?

t_ch: 你还是辞职吧。
t_en: Maybe it's time for you to throw in the towel.

t_ch: 百、百密一疏啊，正常人第一反应都是去查不认识的名字，谁会想到问题出在自己身上？
t_en: I—It's an honest mistake. The reaction of any ordinary person is to check for unfamiliar names, right? Who would think to check their own name?

t_ch: 这涂鸦的位置可真精彩，总觉得她是在跟咱们玩游戏呢，一个点接着一个点……
t_en: The graffiti's location is quite interesting. It feels like she's playing a game with us. One dot leads to another...

t_ch: 真精彩。
t_en: It's really fun.

t_ch: 真难受！
t_en: It's really annoying!

t_ch: 我听说在朋克洛德有一种传统，那儿的骇客在入侵时，都喜欢故意留下点什么，等待着被人发现——就像是一封挑战书。
t_en: I heard from a friend that the hackers on Punklorde have a tradition. When they hack, they purposely leave something behind for people to discover — kind of like throwing down the gauntlet.

t_ch: 他们管这叫「朋克洛德精神」，有交锋才有胜负，有胜负才有乐趣，游戏人生，玩得开心最重要。
t_en: They call this the "Punklorde mentality." There can only be competition when there is rivalry, and there can only be excitement when there is competition. Life's a game and having fun is what's most important.

t_ch: 我是不理解这种想法啦，但现在看来…这些涂鸦可能也是对方故意留下的？为了让我们有迹可循？
t_en: I don't understand that way of thinking, but it seems like this graffiti... might have been left behind for us on purpose as a clue?

t_ch: 上次也是这样，我离目标就只差那么点距离了，结果却在最后的信息攻防中一败涂地…
t_en: The same thing happened last time as well. I was so close to the target but got obliterated in the final battle of information...

t_ch: 这一点差的有点多。
t_en: Oh, so close yet so far.

t_ch: 哎，别提了。还是不要沉浸在过去的失败中了，好好加油，做好眼前事吧！
t_en: *sigh* Doesn't help to dwell on past failures. It's more important to do your best with the things in front of you!

t_ch: 不是你的错。
t_en: It's not your fault.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。呼…好好加油，做好眼前事吧！
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}. Alright, we got this, gotta do our best with the things in front of us!

t_ch: 先专注手头的工作吧！
t_en: Let's focus on the work at hand!

t_ch: 唉，你说的对。还是不要沉浸在过去的失败中了，谢谢你，{NICKNAME}！
t_en: You're right, it doesn't help to dwell on past failures. Thank you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 啊，她看起来要走了，咱们快跟上。
t_en: Ah, she seems to be leaving. Let's keep up.

t_ch: 想都别想。
t_en: That's not gonna happen.

t_ch: 我再说最后一遍，我不关心阮•梅又跟你吹了什么耳旁风，想关停模拟宇宙？门都没有！
t_en: I'll say it again. I don't care what Ruan Mei said to you, but there's no chance we're shutting down the Simulated Universe!

t_ch: 黑塔，这是我的决定。
t_en: Herta, I've made my decision.

t_ch: 提问：我们为这个项目投入了什么？上百个系统时的时间，一整颗星球的资源，还有全宇宙最先进的技术。
t_en: Question: What have we invested into this project? Hundreds of system hours, the resources of an entire planet, and the most advanced technology in the entire universe.

t_ch: 我们又得到了什么？未知，疑惑，和一连串的报错。
t_en: And what have we gotten in return? Unknowns, confusion, and a series of errors.

t_ch: 最初，我们为模拟宇宙加以定义，希望创造一个能够窥探星神行迹的微缩世界。可现在，它正在离这个目标越来越远。
t_en: In the beginning, we defined the Simulated Universe as a miniature world that could be used to discover the traces of the Aeons. But now, it's become something far removed from its original purpose.

t_ch: 「模拟宇宙需要错谬」，这是你自己说的话，现状明明完美符合，你怎么就不满意了？
t_en: "The Simulated Universe is bound to make errors." You said that yourself, and that's exactly what we're experiencing. Why is it a problem all of a sudden?

t_ch: 我欣赏知识的奔流，但不认可模拟宇宙永远只是待办事项上的一纸合约。
t_en: I adore the vast amount of knowledge, but I cannot accept that the Simulated Universe remains simply a pending contract waiting to be attended to.

t_ch: 黑塔，想一想，这个项目，它给了你多少惊喜？再想一想，它给了你多少失望？
t_en: Herta, think about it. How many surprises has this project given you? And how many disappointments has it given you?

t_ch: 模拟宇宙从不会让我失望，让我失望的是你…螺丝。
t_en: The Simulated Universe never disappointed me. You are what disappoints me... Screwllum.

t_ch: 你以为你很了不起吗？你现在就像一个，一个…一个蠢材俱乐部的家伙。
t_en: Do you really think of yourself as someone so exceptionally amazing? Right now, you're more like... like... like someone from the Nitwit Society.

t_ch: 黑塔，我无意质疑你，也不会否定你曾为模拟宇宙做出的努力。
t_en: Herta, I don't mean to question you, nor do I want to deny the hard work you've put forth for the Simulated Universe.

t_ch: 只是，我希望给予知识和灵感更多自由生长的空间。
t_en: I just want to give more room for knowledge and inspiration to grow freely.

t_ch: 够了。
t_en: That's enough.

t_ch: 你不想干，就退出吧，自己去跟剩下两个人讲…哦，把你的技术也一起带走，我不稀罕。
t_en: Leave if you don't want to be a part of this. Go tell the other two yourself... Oh, and take your tech with you. I don't need it.

t_ch: 我可以去找阿德里安•泰勒，可以去找艾普瑟隆的红鼻老人，甚至可以去找博识学会——至少他们不会中途撒手跑路！
t_en: I can go and find Adrian Taylor, or the red-nosed old man from Epsilon, or even the Intelligentsia Guild — at least they won't quit halfway!

t_ch: 螺丝咕姆，我们认识这么久了。我还是第一次……
t_en: Screwllum, we've known each other for a long time. This is the first time I feel that...

t_ch: 觉得你就是块冰冷的铁。
t_en: You're nothing but a piece of ice-cold metal.

t_ch: 抱歉，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}，让你见笑了。
t_en: My apologies, {F#madam}{M#sir}.

t_ch: 发生了什么？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 她又怎么了？
t_en: What's with her?

t_ch: 她也会露出这种表情。
t_en: Never expected to see that kind of expression on her face.

t_ch: 此事因我而起，是我单方面向黑塔提出了终止学术合作的希望。请别误会，我对她和这个项目并无意见。模拟宇宙是项宏伟的实验，黑塔也是一位十足的天才。
t_en: This all started with me — I was the one that brought up the request to end our academic collaboration. Don't get me wrong, I have nothing against Herta or the project. The Simulated Universe is a great experiment and Herta is a true genius.

t_ch: 但我们在研究长远方向上产生了一些分歧，分歧往往带来偏执，而偏执会阻遏知识的形成。
t_en: However, we have our differences when it comes down to our long-term vision for the future. These differences will often manifest into bias, and bias will get in the way of the formulation of knowledge.

t_ch: 所以，我是否应该停止这场已经背离初衷的合作？出于感性，我想要相信黑塔。但客观上：我必须立即执行。
t_en: Deep down, I want to continue to believe in Herta... the question is whether one should end a collaboration that no longer holds true to its initial intent. From an objective standpoint, I should end it immediately.

t_ch: 你在担心她的精神状态吗？
t_en: Are you worried about her mental state?

t_ch: 有一点。
t_en: A little.

t_ch: 以结论而言，是我没有选对正确的时机。
t_en: Judging by the result, I have failed to choose the most suitable time to raise this matter.

t_ch: 完全不。
t_en: Not at all.

t_ch: 很高兴没有给你造成困扰。
t_en: Then I'm glad I haven't caused you vexation on her behalf.

t_ch: 我更担心科员们的精神状态。
t_en: I'm more worried about the mental state of the researchers.

t_ch: 感谢你的幽默，这对紧张的气氛有好处。
t_en: Thank you for the humor. It is good for tense moments like these.

t_ch: 有机生命的情感就像潮汐，是我没看好月亮的方向。让黑塔独处一段时间吧，释放情绪的波动，也有利于我们后续的商谈。
t_en: The emotions of organic life are like tides — it is my fault for not noticing the trajectory of the moon. Let Herta be alone for a while so she can calm her emotions. It'll be beneficial for our negotiations later as well.

t_ch: 至于模拟宇宙…如果你们是为它而来，或许我可以代劳，进行一些解答。
t_en: As for the Simulated Universe... If that's the reason you're here, maybe I can help in answering your questions.

t_ch: （讲述事情经过）
t_en: (Explain what happened)

t_ch: 哦。检索：密林深处的篝火，孤独的石剑指向繁星——「朋克洛德精神」？那可不是普通的奇物，我的朋友。
t_en: Hmm. Search: Bonfire in the depths of the woods. A lone stone sword points to the stars... "Punklorde Mentality"? That is no ordinary curio, my friend.

t_ch: 那是一位巡海游侠写给这个宇宙的情书。{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}，你对朋克洛德有多少了解？
t_en: That is a love letter a Galaxy Ranger has written for the universe. {F#Young madam}{M#Young sir}, how much do you know about Punklorde?

t_ch: 有所耳闻。
t_en: I've heard of it.

t_ch: 没什么印象。
t_en: Don't know much.

t_ch: 没人比我更懂朋克洛德。
t_en: Nobody, and I mean nobody, knows more about Punklorde than I do.

t_ch: 和你沟通真是一种有趣且愉快的体验。
t_en: Hehe, conversing with you is truly a delightful experience.

t_ch: 那是一个由数据与字符构建的星球，在朋克洛德，现实和虚幻的边界同它的昼夜一般模糊。
t_en: It is a planet made of data and symbols. The edges of reality and illusion are blurred in Punklorde, as are its days and nights.

t_ch: 而「以太卡带」，它是朋克洛德的骇客用以编辑现实的芯片，记录了他们认知世界、改写世界的方式。
t_en: An "Aether Cartridge" is a chip that Punklordian hackers use to edit reality. The ways through which a hacker perceives and modifies the world are recorded on the chip.

t_ch: 以太卡带是骇客生命的记录，存在的证明。而被空间站收容的这一张，更属于其中的一位传奇。
t_en: It's a recording of the hacker's life and proof of their existence. The one on the space station belonged to someone truly legendary.

t_ch: 他因机缘巧合成为了一名「巡海游侠」，在漫长的时光中纵横银海，遨游四方，见证过诸多奇人异事。
t_en: He became a Galaxy Ranger by accident and spent many long years traveling among the stars. He encountered countless fascinating individuals and saw wonders witnessed by few.

t_ch: 其中不乏公司和博识学会都未曾记录在案的细节，也因此，它得以成为模拟宇宙庞大演算资料的一部分。
t_en: Many of the records and details recorded therein are beyond the knowledge of even the IPC and the Intelligentsia Guild. That is why it became part of the masses of calculation data for the Simulated Universe.

t_ch: 是的。不用担心，我的朋友，这张卡带从未离开空间站。
t_en: Yes. No need to worry, my friend. The cartridge has never left the space station.

t_ch: 如我方才所说，对于模拟宇宙，它是重要的参考——所以它的数据被提取出来，加入了宇宙模型的演算中。
t_en: It's as I said – it is a very important reference for the Simulated Universe — that is why its data has been extracted and added to the calculations of the universe model.

t_ch: 这想必是黑塔心血来潮的决定，因为没有通知其他人，致使它被列入了下落不明的清单中。
t_en: Herta was probably impulsive and added the cartridge into the simulation without telling anyone, resulting in the item being listed as lost.

t_ch: 原来是虚惊一场。
t_en: So it was a false alarm...?

t_ch: 还是有些担心…
t_en: I'm still a little worried...

t_ch: 对方可能闯入了办公室…
t_en: That person could've gotten into the office...

t_ch: 我理解你的顾虑。倘若对方曾尝试骇入黑塔的办公室，那么模拟宇宙自然会成为她的下一个目标。
t_en: I understand your concern. If the individual in question did indeed try to hack into Herta's office, then the Simulated Universe is undoubtedly her next target.

t_ch: 既然如此，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}，如果你不介意，请允许我为你们的调查献上绵薄之力。
t_en: As such, please allow me to offer my limited abilities to assist you with your investigation, {F#madam}{M#sir}.

t_ch: 如你们所知，模拟宇宙将暂时关闭。在我和黑塔得出确切的结论前，它会一直被封锁，不再开启。
t_en: As you already know, the Simulated Universe will be shut down temporarily. It will remain shut until Herta and I come to a clear conclusion.

t_ch: 是的，现在就是调查卡带数据的最后机会。
t_en: Yes, this is the last chance to investigate the cartridge data.

t_ch: 我会凭借自己在模拟宇宙中的殊异，为你们提供力所能及的帮助，这也是为了表达我单方面关停模拟宇宙的歉意，
t_en: By way of apology for shutting down the Simulated Universe, I will use my abilities in the Universe to provide you with what assistance I can.

t_ch: 这是我的建议，请考虑一下。
t_en: This is my recommendation. Please take your time to consider it.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}，你准备好了吗？
t_en: Well, {F#madam}{M#sir}, are you ready?

t_ch: 我还有些问题…
t_en: I've got something to ask you...

t_ch: 请问吧，我欢迎一切好奇与求知。
t_en: Please, ask away. I welcome all forms of curiosity and the pursuit of knowledge.

t_ch: 真的要关停模拟宇宙吗？
t_en: Are you really shutting down the Simulated Universe?

t_ch: 是的。除了刚才提到的原因，还有一些额外考量。
t_en: Yes. There are other concerns besides those I've mentioned.

t_ch: 我认为模拟宇宙中的奇迹，应当拥有自行生长、萌发的权利，而不是由他人的意志加以干涉，就像宇宙本身。
t_en: I believe that the miracles inside the Simulated Universe have the right to grow and develop organically. The will of others shouldn't be imposed upon it, just like in the actual universe.

t_ch: 黑塔越是殚精竭虑，就越是在远离她所希望的宇宙。结论：日复一日的更迭，正在不断消磨她的才能。
t_en: The more Herta tries, the further she moves away from the Universe that she had hoped for. Conclusion: The endless iterations of the Simulated Universe are wearing down her talents, day by day.

t_ch: 我希望能将挚友从这种状态中解放出来。
t_en: I hope to free my dear friend from it.

t_ch: 但我也要指出，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}，这些都是我的一己之见。对于黑塔，你们应该有自己的判断。
t_en: Nevertheless, I must also clarify that this is my opinion alone. You should form your own judgment on Herta, {F#young madam}{M#young sir}.

t_ch: 你说的巡海游侠是？
t_en: Who are the Galaxy Rangers that you speak of?

t_ch: 是一群随身带着天平的人，他们坚信宇宙间所有的公义须以个人的行为伸张。
t_en: They are a group that carries a balance scale with them at all times. They believe all the justice in the universe needs to be achieved through individual deeds.

t_ch: 那写给宇宙的情书又是？
t_en: Then, what's the love letter for the universe?

t_ch: 这是我的演绎中，最适合巡海游侠的表达。
t_en: That was the most suitable way of describing the Galaxy Rangers in my interpretation.

t_ch: 他们总是乘着歌声与号角，行于「巡猎」的命途之上，为偶遇的个体或群体主持正义，献上美酒与诗章。
t_en: They are directed by the Path of The Hunt and are always accompanied by song and the sound of horns. They pursue justice for the individuals and groups they encounter along the way, and shower them with splendid wines and poems.

t_ch: 但故事终有句点，他们也已消失在聚光灯下了。很久很久。
t_en: However, all stories come to an end, just as they disappeared from the spotlight long ago.

t_ch: 能再讲讲朋克洛德的骇客吗？
t_en: Tell me more about Punklorde's hackers.

t_ch: 那里有一群不知疲惫的生命，试图触摸真理的脐带。
t_en: There is a group of lifeforms there that know nothing of fatigue. They are attempting to touch upon the umbilical cord of the truth.

t_ch: 他们正在把整个星球的语言、思想、乃至感情，编织成无机生命也能理解的字符。
t_en: They are compiling the languages, thoughts, and even emotions of the entire planet into symbols that even inorganic lifeforms can understand.

t_ch: 就像蝴蝶，鲸鱼，和你——在数据的海洋中，生命只有一种形式，通过字段长短的差异，来界定描述的边缘。
t_en: You see, in the vast sea of data, life exists only in one form: A line of code. Whether it's a butterfly, a whale, or even you — the length of that line of code dictates the boundaries of what the entity entails.

t_ch: 原来我是蝴蝶啊。
t_en: So, I'm actually a butterfly.

t_ch: 你在我心中一直是这样的形象。
t_en: I've always thought of you that way.

t_ch: 原来我是鲸鱼啊。
t_en: So, I'm actually a whale.

t_ch: 请勿在意，这只是一个比喻。
t_en: Please, it's only a metaphor.

t_ch: 原来我是数据啊。
t_en: So, I'm actually data.

t_ch: 也许有机生命的喜怒哀乐也是数据的一种表现形式，你不这么认为吗？
t_en: Maybe the emotions of organic lifeforms are an expression of data, don't you think?

t_ch: 没什么想问的了…
t_en: That's all the questions I have...

t_ch: 我们出发吧。
t_en: Let's get going.

t_ch: 嗯，我们走吧。
t_en: Very well, get going we shall.

t_ch: 我会使用有别于常规的方式将你送入模拟宇宙，或许会与你平时的体感有所不同。
t_en: I will be sending you into the Simulated Universe using an alternative method. It might feel a little different than usual.

t_ch: 这能帮助我们在模拟宇宙庞杂的数据中快速定位卡带的位置。从这条数据链入手吧，请做好意识深潜的准备——
t_en: This will help us quickly locate the cartridge in the vast sea of data. We'll start with this data link. Now, please prepare yourself for a deep dive with your consciousness...

t_ch: 还没有准备好…
t_en: I'm not ready yet...

t_ch: 没关系，我会在此等候。
t_en: Take your time, I'll be waiting.

t_ch: 你，你听到了吗？我吓得一句话都不敢说啊！
t_en: D—Did you hear that? I was so scared I couldn't utter a single word!

t_ch: 模拟宇宙要关停？那可是黑塔女士的重点项目啊，要是没了，她会怎么发飙我想都不敢想……
t_en: The Simulated Universe? Shut down? That's Madam Herta's biggest project. I can't imagine how mad she'd be if it fell through...

t_ch: 等一下，这不会是我的错吧？要是我当初能阻止敌人入侵，螺丝咕姆先生是不是就不会这么悲观了？
t_en: Wait... This isn't my fault, is it? If I'd been able to stop the enemy's invasion, would Mr. Screwllum be less pessimistic?

t_ch: 不是你的错。
t_en: It's not your fault.

t_ch: 唉，谢谢你安慰我……
t_en: *sigh* Thanks for trying to comfort me...

t_ch: 每个人都有责任。
t_en: Everyone's responsible.

t_ch: 虽然很无情，但你说得对……
t_en: A bit harsh, but you're right...

t_ch: 还是专注眼前吧。
t_en: Let's focus on what's in front of us.

t_ch: 你说得对……
t_en: You're right...

t_ch: 唔，好不甘心，总觉得我什么也做不到。
t_en: Ugh, this is terrible. I feel like I'm incapable of doing anything...

t_ch: 但继续自责也没用。我们是为卡带来的，至少…得让这件事有个结果。
t_en: ...still, beating myself up all the time won't help either. We're here for the cartridge. At the very least... we must try and see this thing through.

t_ch: 听螺丝咕姆先生的意思，你接下来就要进入模拟宇宙内部调查了吧？
t_en: Having heard what Mr. Screwllum said, you're entering the Simulated Universe to continue the investigation, aren't you?

t_ch: 我大概是没法陪同了。但我会待在办公室里，帮螺丝咕姆先生一起监控系统内部的数据流。好紧张啊，我还是第一次接触这么大的工程。
t_en: I'm afraid I won't be able to accompany you — I'll be helping Mr. Screwllum monitor the system's internal data flow from the office. Ugh, I'm so nervous — this is the first time I've been involved in something so big.

t_ch: 总之，我会为你加油的！
t_en: Anyway, I'll be cheering for you!

t_ch: 怎么了，还没做好心理建设吗？时间不等人啊，朋友，放心跟着螺丝咕姆先生进去吧。
t_en: What is it? Are you still making up your mind? Time doesn't wait, my friend. Put your mind at ease and follow Mr. Screwllum inside.

t_ch: 你回来啦。
t_en: You're back.

t_ch: 你看看办公室牌子上写的是谁的名字，我为什么不可以回来？哼，要走也不是我走，我偏要站这儿。
t_en: Do you not see whose name it is on the desk? Hmph, I can come and go as I wish. Besides, if anyone is going to leave, it definitely won't be me.

t_ch: 怎么，见不得我回来？呵，我才不管你们要做什么，爱干啥干啥，别来烦我就行。
t_en: What, am I not allowed to come back? I really don't care what you guys do. Just don't pester me.

t_ch: 你感到一阵涟漪，巨大的失重感让你失去了四肢的掌控权，在荡漾的字符与代码中，你猛然醒来——这里是模拟宇宙。
t_en: You feel a ripple, a great weightlessness that makes you lose control of your limbs, and in a flurry of symbols and code, you wake up with a jolt — this is the Simulated Universe.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}，你还好吗？
t_en: {F#Young madam}{M#Young sir}, are you alright?

t_ch: 还好。
t_en: I'm fine.

t_ch: 竟然有声音？
t_en: There's sound?

t_ch: 那是通讯模组在正常运作——黑塔常说这是项华而不实的功能，为了提升算力，她通常会将其关闭。
t_en: That's the communication module doing its job — Herta often speaks of it as an expensive and impractical function. She often shuts it off to increase calculating power.

t_ch: 但我相信：比起文字，声音更能承载情感的波动。
t_en: However, I maintain that the voice carries greater emotional charge than the written word.

t_ch: 你在哪儿？
t_en: Where are you?

t_ch: 我在外部，确保系统映射不会崩溃，伦纳德先生也在这里。放心，我们会保障你的安全。
t_en: I'll be staying on the outside — I need to make sure the system projections remain stable. Mr. Leonard is here as well. There's no need to worry. We will guarantee your safety.

t_ch: 现在，你已身处以太卡带所在的数据链中，试着环顾四周吧，告诉我你的发现。
t_en: You are now inside the data link of the Aether Cartridge. Look around and tell me what you discover.

t_ch: 呃，那位骇客就在我面前…
t_en: Uh... that hacker is right in front of me...

t_ch: 我好像出现幻觉了…
t_en: I think I'm hallucinating...

t_ch: 这是正常现象，大多数有机生命在经历信标投射后都会产生不适，你看见了什么？
t_en: That is a normal reaction. Most organic lifeforms will experience discomfort after undergoing beacon projection. What do you see?

t_ch: 那位骇客就在我面前…
t_en: That hacker is right in front of me...

t_ch: 骇客？
t_en: Hacker?
Oh...

t_ch: 结论：那是模拟宇宙生成的全息记录。在这里，每一处变量都会被保存下来，巨细无遗，当然也包括外来者的足迹。
t_en: Conclusion: That is a holographic record that the Simulated Universe has created. In here, regardless of how large or small, all variables are saved, including outsider traces.

t_ch: 你们是对的，敌人的入侵已蔓延至模拟宇宙深处，距离以太卡带所在的数据链只有毫厘之差，我们看到的就是当时的影像。
t_en: You were right. The enemy's invasion has spread to the deepest parts of the Simulated Universe. They were only a hair away from the Aether Cartridge's data link. What we are seeing are the projections of what happened in those moments.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}，请站在原地，让我试着利用一下这份记录吧——
t_en: {F#Young madam}{M#Young sir}, please stay still. Allow me to see what I can do with this record...

t_ch: 「嘿，我说你，怎么一直在盯着我看？」
t_en: "Hey, you, why are you staring at me?"

t_ch: 说话了？！
t_en: It... speaks!?

t_ch: 是真人？
t_en: It's a real person?

t_ch: 你做了什么？
t_en: What did you do?

t_ch: 我推进了模拟宇宙的流程。她并非在和你对话，这只是一段过去的记录。
t_en: I initiated the Simulation flow. This is just a record of the past — she's not actually talking to you.

t_ch: 请先不要打断她，仔细聆听，我们或许能有更多收获。
t_en: Please listen and don't interrupt her. We may be able to find out more of what we need to know.

t_ch: 「首先，我没有朝思暮想。那张卡带的故事在朋克洛德跟教科书没啥两样，这种人尽皆知的东西，我向来没兴趣。」
t_en: "First off, I'm not obsessed with it. On Punklorde, the stories of the cartridge are no different from textbooks. I'm not interested in something that everybody already knows about."

t_ch: 「其次，那不是什么游戏光碟，那可是以太卡带——骇客的第二双眼睛、第二个大脑、第二颗心脏。」
t_en: "Secondly, it's not some kind of video game cartridge. It's an Aether Cartridge — it's the second pair of eyes, second brain, and second heart of the hacker."

t_ch: 「你明白吗，有了这张卡带，你甚至能知道他在电影首映礼上吃的第四粒混合爆米花是什么味道，就是这么厉害。」
t_en: "Do you understand what that actually means? With that cartridge, if a guy went to a movie premiere and bought some mixed popcorn, you'd be able to know the flavor of the fourth kernel he picked out of the bucket. It's truly amazing."

t_ch: 「最后，再强调一遍，我没有朝思暮想，说这么一长串话只是为了跟你解释清楚它到底有多厉害，懂了吗，卡芙卡？」
t_en: "Lastly, I'm gonna say it again — I'm not obsessed with it. The reason I'm still rambling about it is to make sure that you know how amazing it really is. Do you get it now, Kafka?"

t_ch: 「好了，咱们出发吧，动作要快。这里跟刚才的空间站不一样，可是实打实天才俱乐部的地盘。」
t_en: "Alright, let's get going. We have to move quickly. This place is different from the rest of the space station. We're on Genius Society turf."

t_ch: 从这番对话来看，进入模拟宇宙的一共有两人——她，还有她的同伴。
t_en: It is safe to assume that two people entered the Simulated Universe — her, and her companion.

t_ch: 有趣。让我们看看接下来还会发生什么吧？
t_en: Interesting. Let's see what happened next.

t_ch: 我将你编入了同行者的视角，现在，她会对你的行为做出一些反应。
t_en: You are now taking the point of view of the companion. She will react to your behavior now.

t_ch: 「哦，岔路？难不倒我。」
t_en: "Fork in the road, huh? I got this."

t_ch: 「要先挑一个吗？测试一下你最喜欢的…直觉。」
t_en: "Wanna pick one first? Give your beloved instincts a try..."

t_ch: 她在让我做选择？
t_en: Is she telling me to choose?

t_ch: 确切地说，她在让同伴做选择。就算你什么都不做，这份记录也会继续推进。
t_en: Technically, she is asking her companion to choose. The record will continue forward even if you don't take any action.

t_ch: 她话真多。
t_en: She talks a lot.

t_ch: 有位健谈的同行者也不失为一种乐趣，不是吗？
t_en: It's a nice thing to have a companion who likes to talk, isn't it?

t_ch: 这还是…影像记录吗？
t_en: Is this still just... a record?

t_ch: 是的，这仍是一段影像记录，千真万确。
t_en: Yes. This is a visual record. No doubt about it.

t_ch: 「猜错啦——正确答案是右边！」
t_en: "Wrong choice — the correct answer is the path on the right!"

t_ch: 「啊？怎么不信邪呢，都说了是右边，右边！」
t_en: "What? Why won't you believe me? I told you, it's the right side, the right!"

t_ch: 「错了，这是个诱饵信号。」
t_en: "Hmm, not what we're looking for. It's a bait signal."

t_ch: 「黑塔这家伙，竟然在模拟宇宙里布置这种东西，她自己找资料时不会嫌麻烦吗？」
t_en: "Ugh, why would Herta put something like this inside the Simulated Universe? Doesn't she find it annoying when she has to find something herself?"

t_ch: 「算了算了，是我计算失误，咱们再换个扇区试试。」
t_en: "Never mind, I miscalculated. Let's try another sector."

t_ch: 似乎不太顺利啊。
t_en: It doesn't seem to be going well.

t_ch: 其实她走在正确的路上，只是走得还不够远——相信用不了多久，她就会意识到这点。
t_en: She's actually on the correct path. She just needs to continue a little further — I trust that she too will realize this soon.

t_ch: 但愿她发现不了…
t_en: I hope she doesn't...

t_ch: 希望如此，但客观来看：她一定会发现。
t_en: Indeed. However, objectively speaking, it is almost certain that she will.

t_ch: 为什么这么肯定？
t_en: Why are you so sure?

t_ch: 已有之事，后必再有——这是在与她的接触中，我做出的推断。
t_en: What has happened is destined to happen again — that was my conclusion after having met her.

t_ch: 你似乎很了解她？
t_en: You seem to know her really well.

t_ch: 只是有过一面之缘。
t_en: We've met only once.

t_ch: 星核猎手「银狼」，我们曾有过一段交锋，当时黑塔也在场——她或许向你提起过此事。
t_en: Stellaron Hunter, Silver Wolf. We've fought against one another before. Herta was there as well — she may have mentioned it to you.

t_ch: 记得是你赢了…
t_en: I recall that you won...

t_ch: 在我看来，那并不算胜利。
t_en: From my point of view, it wasn't a victory.

t_ch: 记得是她赢了…
t_en: I recall that she won...

t_ch: 用黑塔的话说，那场对局没有输家。
t_en: In Herta's words, there was no loser in that fight.

t_ch: 记得是平手…
t_en: I recall that it was a draw...

t_ch: 比起对决，那更像一次试探。她进攻，我防守，不分胜负，双方都有所保留。她没能攻破差分机球的防御，我也未阻止她扬长而去。
t_en: It was more like a test than a duel. She would attack, and I would defend — neither of us gave it our all. She wasn't able to break through my defenses and I wasn't able to stop her from leaving.

t_ch: 没想到今日会以这种形式再见。相信上一场交锋的延续很快就会到来。
t_en: I never thought our next meeting would take place under these circumstances. I believe the continuation of our last encounter will soon arrive.

t_ch: 「搞什么，这条路明明是对的嘛。黑塔这家伙，还玩心理战术啊。」
t_en: "What the... This was the right way all along. Ugh, Herta's got some psychological tactics going on."

t_ch: 看来，我们可以继续前进了。
t_en: Seems like we can now continue forward.

t_ch: 「喔，又回到空间站了。还有解谜？一键跳过吧。」
t_en: "Oh, back to the space station again. A riddle? Where's the skip button?"

t_ch: 「总算快到终点了。笑死，这儿还有个黑塔小人。」
t_en: "Finally, almost there. Ha, there's even a miniature Herta over here."

t_ch: 「这人自恋吧？整个空间站都是她的脸——画像，雕塑，还有一大堆投影。」
t_en: "A little narcissistic, isn't she? The whole space station is filled with her face — portraits, statues, even projections of herself."

t_ch: 「我本打算给她脸上加个小胡子的，结果竟然喷不上去——太离谱了吧，整个空间站都跟纸糊的一样，只有她的照片密不透风？」
t_en: "I was planning on adding a mustache for her but the spray didn't work — the entire space station feels like it's made of paper but her portrait is near indestructible."

t_ch: 是说电梯间那幅画像吧？黑塔曾委托我为她设计一种加密工具，原来是用在了这里。
t_en: Hehe, she's talking about that portrait in the elevator room, no? Herta commissioned me to design an encryption tool for her — seems she ended up using it on her portrait.

t_ch: 她说快到终点了？
t_en: She said she's almost at the destination?

t_ch: 没错。以太卡带的数据就在下一个房间，我们的追踪也快要结束了。
t_en: That's correct. The Aether Cartridge data is in the next room. Our pursuit is coming to an end.

t_ch: 有种奇怪的感觉…
t_en: I have a strange feeling about this...

t_ch: 有种奇妙的感觉…
t_en: I have a wonderful feeling about this...

t_ch: 是的。在漫长的追踪中，这种违和感贯穿始终。
t_en: Yes. That incongruous feeling has persisted throughout this long pursuit.

t_ch: 她固然是为星核而来，却也对奇物表现出了浓厚的兴趣，而在我们意识到以太卡带的存在后，她又恰到好处地出现在了模拟宇宙中。
t_en: She came for the Stellaron but showed a strong interest in the curios... And just as we found out about the existence of the Aether Cartridge, she just so happened to show up in the Simulated Universe.

t_ch: 仿佛不是我们在追踪她，而是她出现在我们行进的路径上。
t_en: I must admit, this feels less like a chase and more like a chance encounter.

t_ch: 我想起一个故事，听闻在朋克洛德，涂鸦是一种特别的符号。骇客将现实视作一场宏大的游戏，追寻着生命的「通关」。
t_en: I recall a story about Punklorde. It is said that graffiti is a special kind of symbol there. Hackers see reality as a magnificent game and attempt to finish the stage we know as "life."

t_ch: 或是为了胜负，或是为了喜悦，他们在走过的地方留下自己的印记，一个又一个图案，串联起漫长的旅途，编写下生命的游戏。
t_en: Whether it be for competition or for joy, they leave a trail in the places they visit. One symbol after another link up to become a long, long journey that writes the game of life.

t_ch: 不觉得和现状很像吗？也许她费尽心思，就只是为了和我们玩一场孩童的游戏。
t_en: And would this ongoing affair not be part of such an endeavour? Perhaps this is all nothing more than a game she's decided to play with us...

t_ch: 就让我们陪她玩到最后吧。
t_en: ...in which case — let us accompany her till the end.

t_ch: 看，我们找到了，以太卡带就在这里，完好无损。
t_en: Look, we found it. The Aether Cartridge is here and it's perfectly intact.

t_ch: 可是…
t_en: But...

t_ch: 有点奇怪…
t_en: Something's not right...

t_ch: 这是当然，毕竟，这里还有一位不合时宜的第三者。
t_en: There is an unwelcome third party here with us.

t_ch: 假设我们看到的银狼是过去的影像，她一定不可能抵达这里，否则卡带就不会安然无恙。所以假设与事实产生了矛盾。
t_en: If what we are seeing constitutes past images of Silver Wolf, then for her to have made it this far would've entailed the disappearance of the cartridge. We can only assume that the facts don't align with the theory.

t_ch: 我想，她当时确实骇入了黑塔的办公室，但并未在其中停留太久。
t_en: I believe that she was indeed able to hack into Herta's office, but she didn't stay there for very long.

t_ch: 毕竟面对模拟宇宙浩若星海的数据，就算是顶尖的骇客也无法在短时间内找到特定的目标，除非……
t_en: After all, even the best hacker wouldn't be able to find a specified target in the vast ocean of the Simulated Universe's data. Unless...

t_ch: 「除非，有一位合适的向导。」
t_en: "Unless she had someone to guide her there."

t_ch: 说话了？！
t_en: It... speaks!?

t_ch: 果然…
t_en: Of course...

t_ch: 「哈，要不要先和边上这位解释下发生了什么，我看{F#她}{M#他}已经满头问号了哦？」
t_en: "Ha, you wanna explain it to {F#her}{M#him} first? I can see the confusion in {F#her}{M#his} eyes."

t_ch: 看，{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}，最后的结果已经摆上展台，你可以仔细端详。今天的时间还有许多，我可以回答你的任何问题。
t_en: The final results are on the display table, {F#young madam}{M#young sir}. Take a closer look if you wish, we have plenty of time left. I will answer any questions that you may have.

t_ch: 所以，她其实是…
t_en: So she's actually...

t_ch: 正确的猜测。在你面前的，就是今晚的主角。
t_en: You've guessed correctly. The person that stands before you is tonight's starring role.

t_ch: 她在办公室留下了一道后门，意图在你进入模拟宇宙的同时远程骇入，然后以某种方式与我们同行。
t_en: She left herself a back door in the office, performed a proxy hack the moment you entered the Simulated Universe, and found a way to continue alongside us.

t_ch: 不得不说，她也是一位技艺精湛的演员，混淆视听永远是最有效的方法。
t_en: I must say, she really is a masterful actor. Misdirection is always the most effective method.

t_ch: 果然，那些涂鸦是诱饵？
t_en: So, the graffiti was bait?

t_ch: 涂鸦就像是数据的双向通道。你们能以此回溯她留下的痕迹，她也能通过涂鸦的存在与消失监测你们的进度。
t_en: The graffiti is like a two-way data tunnel. You can use it to retrace the trail she leaves behind, and she can monitor your progress by seeing which pieces of graffiti have disappeared.

t_ch: 恐怕对方在入侵空间站时就已经想好了整个计划，布置涂鸦，留下后门，都是为了静待机会来临。
t_en: I fear she may well have had the whole thing planned out before hacking the space station. The graffiti and the back door were all in aid of honing in on just the right moment.

t_ch: 你们从一开始就知道？
t_en: You knew everything from the start?

t_ch: 抱歉，黑塔禁止我提前透露任何信息。她说在这个计划中，重要的不是智慧，而是无知。
t_en: I'm sorry — Herta forbade me from disclosing any information beforehand. She made clear that knowledge would not be paramount to this operation, but rather the lack of it.

t_ch: 但在我看来，最重要的——是执着与真诚。毫无疑问，伦纳德先生的一举一动完全吸引了对方。
t_en: However, the way I see it, the most important elements were dedication and sincerity — the actions of Mr. Leonard drew the target right in.

t_ch: 我没有问题了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 那么，对这场模拟宇宙之旅，您可还满意——
t_en: Well then, were you satisfied with this Simulated Universe journey...

t_ch: 银狼小姐？
t_en: ...Miss Silver Wolf?

t_ch: 哼，你早就发现了啊。
t_en: Hmph, you knew it all along, didn't you?

t_ch: 我就知道想瞒过你没那么简单，一路上那些闲言碎语，都是说给我听的，对不？
t_en: I knew it would be hard to fool you. All that talk you were spouting along the way was meant for me, wasn't it?

t_ch: 再次与您交手，令人喜悦又荣幸。黑塔说这张卡带可以钓起银河里的大鱼——她的判断总是准确的。
t_en: It is a joy and an honor to have the opportunity to face off against you once again. Herta said that this cartridge would be able to lure in the biggest fishes in the universe — she was right.

t_ch: 所以什么关停宇宙，和黑塔的争执，都是在演戏？就是为了请我入瓮？
t_en: So, the business about shutting down the Universe and the argument with Herta was all just an act? You did all that to lure me in?

t_ch: 这取决于您的理解，我和黑塔向来有话直言，这样尖锐的交锋实属常有——说它完全是表演，也不妥当。
t_en: That is up to you to interpret — Herta and I have always been frank with one another. Sharp conversations of that nature happen on a regular basis. It wouldn't be fitting to call it an act.

t_ch: 哼。
t_en: Hmph.

t_ch: 看，银狼小姐着实是位才华横溢的骇客，她计算一切，留足了变量与后手。
t_en: Miss Silver Wolf is truly a talented hacker. Not only did she calculate everything ahead of time, she has numerous variables and aces still to play.

t_ch: 恐怕在她的计划中，唯一的变数就是我突然拜访空间站的消息——随之而来的，还有模拟宇宙将要被关停的噩耗。这会让她的布局功亏一篑。
t_en: News of my sudden visit to the space station was probably the only uncontrollable variable in her plan — and with my arrival came the nightmare of shutting down the Simulated Universe. That would have ruined her plans and all her preparations.

t_ch: 所以她不得不冒险。
t_en: That's why she had to take the risk.

t_ch: 所以她必须抓住机会。
t_en: That's why she had to take the opportunity before it was lost.

t_ch: 至此，你已经明白事件的全貌了。
t_en: You now know everything about this encounter.

t_ch: 全貌？不不，你弄错了最关键的一点。
t_en: Everything? No, no, you're wrong about the most important part.

t_ch: 我不是铤而走险，而是跃跃欲试，明白吗？螺丝咕姆，你知道你出现的那一刻，我有多兴奋吗。
t_en: You think I came here out of desperation, but I've been bursting with excitement this whole time. Do you know how happy I was when you showed up, Screwllum?

t_ch: 一个旗鼓相当的对手，我有多久没有享受过这种乐趣啦——军团是群原始人，公司也差点意思，只有俱乐部能给我一些乐子。
t_en: I haven't had the thrill of a formidable opponent in such a long time... The Legion is nothing but a bunch of cavemen, and the IPC were a disappointment. Only the Society has the talent to provide me with a fun challenge.

t_ch: 现实是一场游戏——可如果不能开心，游戏又有什么意义？两位天才携手向我递出了一份邀请函，一封挑战书，我怎能不欣然接受——换作是你，你能吗？
t_en: Reality is nothing but a game... but what's the point of a game if it can't make you happy? An invitation letter and a challenge letter were sent to me from two geniuses. How could I not accept them? Would you have turned them down?

t_ch: 您确信自己能全身而退。
t_en: You're confident you will be able to get out of this in one piece.

t_ch: 没错，还要带着它一起。
t_en: That's right, and I'm bringing this with me.

t_ch: 以太卡带？
t_en: The Aether Cartridge?

t_ch: 什么时候…
t_en: When did you...

t_ch: 哦，一份复制品，是在刚才的对话过程中完成的？
t_en: Oh, a copy. You completed it while we were talking just now?

t_ch: 不然为什么陪你们说这么多开场白？在拖延时间的可不止你。我猜黑塔正在哪个角落沿着我入侵的通道反向骇入，而你负责留在这里和我正面攻防，就像是上次对决的重演。
t_en: Why else would I entertain so much small talk? You're not the only one stalling for time. I'm guessing that Herta is hiding in a corner somewhere trying to pull off a reverse hack from where I hacked in. You, on the other hand, are responsible for taking me head-on, just as you did last time.

t_ch: 好啊，我很期待哦！这次你们准备怎么阻止我呢？把模拟宇宙当成黑匣子？联合公司进行跨星系包围？螺丝咕姆，我能见识到你真正的实力吗？
t_en: And I'm looking forward to it! How do you plan on stopping me this time? Are you going to turn the Simulated Universe into a black box? Or are you going to work with the IPC and put together a cross-galactic encirclement? Screwllum, will I be able to see your true strength this time?

t_ch: 没能分出结果的胜负终于要成为历史了，今天胜者只会有一个。拜托了，让我玩得开心点，好吗？
t_en: The long-awaited result will finally be determined. There will only be one victor today. Please... let me have some fun this time, okay?

t_ch: 抱歉，银狼小姐。您期待的这些都不会发生。现在，您可以离开了。
t_en: My apologies, Miss Silver Wolf, but none of what you are hoping for will be happening today. You may leave now.

t_ch: 哈？
t_en: Huh?

t_ch: 离开？…为什么？不是要抓捕我吗，给我戴上镣铐，扔到遥远的流放之地？
t_en: Leave? But... why? Aren't you trying to catch me? Don't you want to cuff me and exile me on some distant planet?

t_ch: 您的想象很有趣。然而您和空间站的恩怨与我无关，逻辑：我不会阻止。
t_en: Your creativity has proven interesting. However, I have nothing to do with your quarrel with the space station. Ergo: I won't stop you.

t_ch: 那我可要带走这张卡带啦。
t_en: I'm gonna leave with this cartridge then.

t_ch: 那只是一份复制而已。更正：不止您手中那张，存放在这里的也是。真正的奇物在计划之初就转移走了。结论：您可以自由分享这份数据。
t_en: That's just a duplicate. Correction: Not only is the one in your possession a duplicate, but so is the one here. The real curio was transferred elsewhere at the very beginning. Conclusion: You may share that data freely.

t_ch: 我可能会再次进攻空间站。
t_en: What if I attack the space station again?

t_ch: 这与我无关。但我要指出：推测会有人站出来阻止您。
t_en: It would have nothing to do with me. But I must warn you: It is likely that someone would step forward to stop you.

t_ch: 那…要是我当场摧毁模拟宇宙呢？
t_en: Then... What if I were to destroy the Simulated Universe right now?

t_ch: 抱歉，您做不到。以太编辑不是万能的，并不能毁灭一个宇宙。
t_en: I'm sorry, but that simply isn't possible. Aether editing isn't capable of destroying an entire universe.

t_ch: 这算什么，你们花这么大力气布置了一个陷阱，却什么也不打算做？
t_en: What is the point of this then? You guys went out of your way to put together this trap but don't plan on doing anything at all?

t_ch: 是的。这下子，您费尽心思准备的这场游戏才算是「功亏一篑」了，不是吗？只要没有乐趣，游戏就再无意义。
t_en: That's right. This way, the game that you so carefully orchestrated is now "pointless," is it not? There's no point in a game if it's no longer fun.

t_ch: 银狼，我知道你的诉求。公司的镣铐关不住你，监牢不过是又一片游戏的舞台。黑塔和我达成共识：不会再迁就你的需要了。
t_en: Silver Wolf, I know what you're after. The IPC's shackles won't be able to hold you and jail is but another game to you. Herta and I came to an understanding: We will no longer enable you and give you what you need.

t_ch: 真无聊！
t_en: How boring!

t_ch: 不过，黑塔觉得这结果太过温柔，想要再添几笔。比如…此刻，她通过反向骇入，定位了你所有的星际网络账号——总计<unbreak>76</unbreak>个，很可观的数字。
t_en: However, Herta felt that this would be too easy on you and wanted to add something of her own. And that is why, in this very moment, she has reverse-hacked you and located all of your Interastral Network accounts — a total of <unbreak>76</unbreak> accounts. A very impressive number.

t_ch: 让公司将它们全部冻结如何？连同其中的游戏记录一起。
t_en: How would it be if the IPC were to freeze all of them? Including all your game records?

t_ch: 什么！喂，等等——
t_en: What!? Hey, hold on a minute!

t_ch: …退出了，真心急啊。
t_en: ...Aaaand she logged out. How hasty of her.

t_ch: 孩子眼中的游戏当然是快乐的，但对于大人，游戏并不是解决问题的唯一方式。
t_en: *sigh* Games are always fun in the eyes of a child... but to an adult, they are but one among many ways of solving a problem.

t_ch: 我们也离开这里吧。
t_en: It's about time we were on our way.

t_ch: 哼，大获全胜——小家伙，现在知道黑塔的厉害了吧。
t_en: Hmph, a flawless victory — hope you caught a glimpse of Herta's awesomeness, kiddo.

t_ch: 一切都在你的计划中吗？
t_en: Was it all part of your plan?

t_ch: 那是自然。
t_en: Naturally.

t_ch: 不愧是黑塔大人。
t_en: As expected of Madam Herta.

t_ch: 知道就好。
t_en: Mhm, make sure you never forget about it.

t_ch: 何必搞得这么复杂。
t_en: Why make it so complicated?

t_ch: 这还复杂，你当朋克洛德的骇客都是傻瓜吗？
t_en: You think this was complicated? Do you think the Punklorde hackers are amateurs?

t_ch: 空间站的事我不管，但敢挑衅我的人，我必当百倍奉还。
t_en: I couldn't care less about what happened with the space station, but I will surely repay the favor tenfold to anyone who dares to taunt me.

t_ch: 已经让公司的家伙把账号全部冻结了，估计那小姑娘现在正抱着电脑痛哭流涕吧，哈哈！
t_en: I already notified someone at the IPC to freeze all her accounts. She's probably crying in front of her computer right now. Hahaha!
